member.name: T:WebAPI.Controllers.V1.UserManagerController
member.summary: Quản lý người dùng (Chỉ có người dùng có quyền mới được vào đây)
member.remarks: This controller enforces business rules to prevent modification or deletion of protected users and roles, such as SuperRoles and users listed in the protected configuration. All endpoints require specific permissions and may return error responses if protection rules are violated. API versioning is supported via the route template. Thread safety is managed by ASP.NET Core's request handling; concurrent requests may result in race conditions if user or role state changes rapidly.
member.param: The MediatR mediator used to send queries and commands.

member.name: T:WebAPI.Converters.EmptyStringConverter
member.summary: Provides a custom JSON converter for strings that substitutes empty strings for null values during deserialization and omits empty strings during serialization.
member.remarks: Use this converter to ensure that deserialized string properties are never null, but instead default to an empty string. When serializing, properties with null or empty string values are not written to the JSON output. This behavior can help avoid null reference issues and reduce JSON payload size for optional string properties.

member.name: M:WebAPI.Converters.EmptyStringConverter.Read(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonReader@,System.Type,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)
member.summary: Reads a JSON string value from the specified reader and returns it as a .NET string.
member.param: A reference to the positioned at the JSON value to read. The reader must be at a string token.
The type of the value to convert. This parameter is ignored for string values.
Options to control the behavior of the JSON serializer. This parameter is not used for string values.
member.returns: The string value read from the JSON input. Returns if the JSON value is null.

member.name: M:WebAPI.Converters.EmptyStringConverter.Write(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonWriter,System.String,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)
member.summary: Writes the specified string value to the JSON output using the provided writer, if the value is not null or empty.
member.param: The instance used to write the JSON value.
The string value to write. If null or empty, no value is written.
Options to control JSON serialization behavior. This parameter can be used to customize serialization features.

member.name: M:WebAPI.Converters.EmptyStringConverter.WriteAsPropertyName(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonWriter,System.String,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)
member.summary: Writes the string value as a JSON property name.

member.name: M:WebAPI.Converters.EmptyStringConverter.ReadAsPropertyName(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonReader@,System.Type,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)
member.summary: Reads the string value as a JSON property name.

member.name: T:WebAPI.Extensions.AuthenticationExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for configuring JWT bearer authentication in an ASP.NET Core application.
member.remarks: This class contains methods that extend the functionality of IServiceCollection to simplify the setup of JWT authentication using configuration values. It is intended to be used during application startup when configuring authentication services.

member.name: M:WebAPI.Extensions.AuthenticationExtensions.AddJwtAuthentication(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection,Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.IConfiguration)
member.summary: Adds JWT bearer authentication to the application's service collection using configuration settings.
member.remarks: This method configures JWT authentication with validation for issuer, audience, lifetime, and signing key. The relevant JWT settings must be present in the configuration under the 'Jwt' section. The method sets the default authentication scheme to JWT bearer.
member.param: The service collection to which authentication services will be added.
The application configuration containing JWT settings such as issuer, audience, and signing key.
member.returns: The same instance so that additional calls can be chained.
member.exception: Thrown if the required JWT signing key ('Jwt:Key') is missing from the configuration.

member.name: T:WebAPI.Extensions.MigrationExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for applying database migrations and seeding initial data to an ASP.NET Core application at startup.
member.remarks: These extensions are intended to be used during application startup to ensure the database schema is up to date and required seed data is present. The seeding operation is controlled by the configuration setting 'SeedingOptions:RunDataSeedingOnStartup'. If enabled, roles, users, permissions, and protected entities are seeded using the application's registered services. This class is typically used in the Program.cs file as part of the application's initialization pipeline.

member.name: M:WebAPI.Extensions.MigrationExtensions.ApplyMigrationsAndSeedAsync(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.WebApplication,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Applies any pending database migrations and optionally seeds initial data during application startup.
member.remarks: Data seeding is performed only if the configuration value 'SeedingOptions:RunDataSeedingOnStartup' is set to . This method should be called during application startup to ensure the database schema is up to date and required data is present.
member.param: The current instance to which migrations and seeding will be applied.
A cancellation token that can be used to cancel the migration and seeding operations.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation.

member.name: T:WebAPI.Extensions.MvcExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for configuring MVC services with custom conventions, API versioning, validation error handling, and JSON serialization options in an ASP.NET Core application.
member.remarks: This static class is intended to be used in the application's service registration phase, typically within the Startup class or Program file. The provided extension methods streamline the setup of common MVC features, including routing conventions, API versioning, standardized validation error responses, global exception handling, and JSON serialization settings. Using these extensions helps ensure consistent behavior and reduces boilerplate code across the application.

member.name: M:WebAPI.Extensions.MvcExtensions.AddCustomMvc(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection)
member.summary: Adds and configures custom MVC, API versioning, routing, validation response, exception handling, and JSON serialization settings to the application's service collection.
member.remarks: This method configures routing to use lowercase URLs and query strings, sets up API versioning with URL segment support, customizes validation error responses, registers a global exception handler, and applies JSON serialization options. It is intended to be called during application startup to standardize API behavior across the application.
member.param: The to which the MVC and related services will be added. Cannot be null.
member.returns: The same instance so that additional calls can be chained.

member.name: T:WebAPI.Extensions.ObservabilityExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for configuring OpenTelemetry tracing and metrics with custom service information in an ASP.NET Core application.
member.remarks: This static class contains extension methods that simplify the setup of OpenTelemetry instrumentation and exporting for services. The extensions are intended to be used during application startup to enable distributed tracing and metrics collection with minimal configuration. All methods are thread-safe and designed for use in dependency injection scenarios.

member.name: M:WebAPI.Extensions.ObservabilityExtensions.AddCustomLogging(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection,Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.IConfiguration,System.String)
member.summary: Adds custom logging using Serilog and Grafana Loki to the service collection.
member.param: The service collection to which logging services will be added.
The application configuration used to retrieve Loki settings.
The name of the service to be used for logging identification.
member.returns: The same service collection instance with Serilog configured.
member.exception: Thrown if services or configuration is null.

member.name: M:WebAPI.Extensions.ObservabilityExtensions.AddCustomOpenTelemetry(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection,Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.IConfiguration,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Adds OpenTelemetry tracing and metrics to the service collection using the specified configuration, service name, and service version.
member.remarks: This method configures both tracing and metrics for ASP.NET Core and HTTP client instrumentation, sets up resource information, and exports telemetry data using OTLP and Prometheus exporters. The OTLP endpoint is read from the configuration key 'OpenTelemetry:OtlpEndpoint'; if not specified, it defaults to 'http://localhost:4317'.
member.param: The service collection to which OpenTelemetry services will be added.
The application configuration used to retrieve OpenTelemetry settings, such as the OTLP endpoint.
The name of the service to be used for OpenTelemetry resource identification and instrumentation.
The version of the service to be used for OpenTelemetry resource identification.
member.returns: The same service collection instance with OpenTelemetry tracing and metrics configured.

member.name: T:WebAPI.Extensions.RateLimitingExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for configuring rate limiting services.

member.name: M:WebAPI.Extensions.RateLimitingExtensions.AddRateLimitingServices(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection)
member.summary: Adds rate limiting services to the specified . Loopback (localhost) requests are excluded from rate limiting.
member.param: The service collection to add rate limiting to.

member.name: T:WebAPI.Extensions.SwaggerExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for configuring custom Swagger/OpenAPI services in an ASP.NET Core application.
member.remarks: This class contains static methods that extend the service collection to add Swagger generation and configuration, including JWT bearer authentication support and XML documentation integration. Use these extensions during application startup to enable API documentation and security definitions for your web APIs.

member.name: M:WebAPI.Extensions.SwaggerExtensions.AddCustomSwagger(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection,Microsoft.AspNetCore.Hosting.IWebHostEnvironment)
member.summary: Adds and configures Swagger services for API documentation, including JWT bearer authentication support and XML comments integration based on the hosting environment.
member.remarks: XML comments are included in the Swagger documentation only if the environment is not set to "Test" and the XML file exists. JWT bearer authentication is configured for API security. This method enables Swagger annotations and registers additional Swagger options.
member.param: The service collection to which Swagger services will be added.
The current web hosting environment, used to determine whether to include XML comments in the Swagger configuration.
member.returns: The same service collection instance, allowing for method chaining.

member.name: T:WebAPI.Middleware.GlobalExceptionHandler
member.summary: Provides a centralized exception handler for HTTP requests, generating standardized API error responses and logging exceptions.
member.remarks: This handler formats error responses as JSON and distinguishes between validation errors and unhandled exceptions. In development environments, detailed exception information is included in the response; in production, a generic error message is returned. All exceptions are logged appropriately based on their type.
member.param: The hosting environment used to determine whether detailed error information should be included in responses.
The logger used to record exception details and validation failures.

member.name: M:WebAPI.Middleware.GlobalExceptionHandler.#ctor(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Hosting.IWebHostEnvironment,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger{WebAPI.Middleware.GlobalExceptionHandler})
member.summary: Provides a centralized exception handler for HTTP requests, generating standardized API error responses and logging exceptions.
member.remarks: This handler formats error responses as JSON and distinguishes between validation errors and unhandled exceptions. In development environments, detailed exception information is included in the response; in production, a generic error message is returned. All exceptions are logged appropriately based on their type.
member.param: The hosting environment used to determine whether detailed error information should be included in responses.
The logger used to record exception details and validation failures.

member.name: M:WebAPI.Middleware.GlobalExceptionHandler.TryHandleAsync(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http.HttpContext,System.Exception,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Attempts to handle the specified exception by writing an appropriate error response to the HTTP context asynchronously.
member.remarks: If the exception is a validation error, a detailed validation error response is returned with status code 400 (Bad Request). For other exceptions, a generic error response is returned with status code 500 (Internal Server Error). The response format and content type are set to JSON. The method does not propagate the exception further.
member.param: The HTTP context to which the error response will be written. Must not be null.
The exception to handle and convert into an error response. Must not be null.
A cancellation token that can be used to cancel the asynchronous operation.
member.returns: A value indicating whether the exception was handled and an error response was written. Always returns .
